---
name: forget
description: Forget Synapsys memories. Use when the user says "forget X", "don't remember Y anymore", "delete this memory", "remove memory", "drop the X memory", or asks to clean up old memories. Archives (soft-delete) memory files to <store>/_archive/<name>.<timestamp>.md — recoverable, never `rm`.
argument-hint: '[memory-name...] | [--all-from=<kind>]'
user-invocable: true
allowed-tools: Bash, AskUserQuestion
---

<!-- codex:plugin-root-preamble v1 — generated by scripts/codemod-plugin-root-preamble.js; edit the codemod, then re-run -->

> **Resolve the plugin root before running any script below.** A codex shell
> can inherit a stale, set-but-WRONG `CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT` from its launcher,
> so a bare `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT:-…}` fallback is NOT safe — validate the
> value against this skill's own SKILL.md first. Set `SKILL_MD` to this
> file's absolute path (codex lists it next to this skill in your skills
> list; under Claude Code the env var is always correct and the fallback
> branch never runs):
>
> ```bash
> SKILL_MD="/absolute/path/to/this/SKILL.md"   # from your skills list
> if [ -f "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT:-/nonexistent}/skills/forget/SKILL.md" ] &&
>    [ "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/skills/forget/SKILL.md" -ef "$SKILL_MD" ]; then
>   PLUGIN_ROOT="$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT"                         # validated — use as-is
> else
>   PLUGIN_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$SKILL_MD")/../.." && pwd)" # stale/unset — self-locate
> fi
> ```
>
> Wherever this skill writes `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}`, substitute the validated
> `$PLUGIN_ROOT` value (shells do NOT share variables between tool calls —
> re-resolve or inline the resolved path in each command).

<!-- codex:arguments-note v1 — generated by scripts/codemod-plugin-root-preamble.js -->

> **`$ARGUMENTS` under codex**: there is no argument substitution — the
> argument (e.g. the ticket id) is the text after the `$forget` skill
> mention in the user's message.

# Forget

## Decision logic

1. **If user passed memory names or `--all-from=<kind>` as args**: invoke the script directly and print its output. Done.

   ```bash
   node "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/synapsys-forget.js" $ARGUMENTS
   ```

2. **If no args**: get the inventory as JSON (no deletion):
   ```bash
   node "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/synapsys-forget.js" --list
   ```

   Parse the JSON. Present memories via `AskUserQuestion` (multi-select). Show `name — description` per option; group by store kind if multiple stores. Then call the script with the chosen names:

   ```bash
   node "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/synapsys-forget.js" <name1> <name2> ...
   ```

The script archives to `<store>/_archive/<name>.<YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS>.md` (never `rm`). The `_archive/` folder is ignored by the dispatcher hook because it scans only the store root for `.md` files.

To restore: the user moves the file back manually (`/forget` deliberately does NOT auto-restore — it would defeat the safety net).

Never delete `.synapsys.json` (the store marker); the script will refuse names that aren't actual memories.
